sections.paragraph:
fut. יֶאֱרֹב-

(1) . to knot, to weave, to intertwine, whence אֲרֻבָּה a net, net-work. (Kindred is עָרַב to mingle. Arab. أَرَبَ to tie a knot, II. id., أَرْبَةُ a knot.)

(2) to lie in wait. (Arab. أَرِبَ to be cunning, astute, III. to act cunningly (prop. intricately). Verbs of intertwining, weaving, also of twisting, spinning, are often applied to craftiness and snares, and are opposed to upright and open course of acting. Comp. עָמַר, פָּתַל, Gr. δόλον, μῆτιν ὑφαίνειν, κακά, δόλον ῥάπτειν, nectere insidias, scelera, suere dolos, Germ. Trug fpinnen, anzetteln.) Constr. followed by לְ, Psa 59:4 Pro 24:15 Jos 8:4 followed by an acc., Pro 12:6 עַל Jud. 9:34 . Elsewhere put absolutely, for to watch in ambush, Jud. 9:34 21:20 followed by a gerund, Pro. loc. cit.; Psa 10:9. Part. אֹורֵב, הָאֹורֵב a lier in wait, often coll. liers in wait, a body of soldiers set in ambush, Jos 8:14, 19 Jos 8:19, 21 Jos 8:21 Jud. 20:33 , seq.; hence construed with a plur., loc. cit. verse 37 Jdg 20:37.

Piel, i.q. Kal, construed followed by עַל, 2Ch 20:22, absol. Jud. 9:25 .

Hiphil, to set an ambush. Fut. וַיָּרֶב for וַיַּאֲרֶב, 1Sa 15:5.

The derivatives follow, except מַאֲרָב.
